title: Multipart File Upload
overview: Upload files via API using multipart form data in REST Assured.
whyItMatters: Many applications allow users to upload files (profile pictures, documents). Testing these endpoints requires simulating multipart/form-data HTTP requests.
explanation: REST Assured handles file uploads using the `multiPart()` method. You typically provide the form control name (as expected by the server) and a `java.io.File` object. Behind the scenes, REST Assured automatically sets the `Content-Type` to `multipart/form-data` and handles the boundary generation. You can chain multiple `multiPart()` calls to send files along with standard form fields.
keyPoints: Use `.multiPart("controlName", new File("path"))` to attach a file.
REST Assured defaults the content type to `multipart/form-data` when using `multiPart`.
You can combine files and text fields by chaining `multiPart` calls.
Test files should be placed in `src/test/resources` for reliable relative path resolution.
codeExample:
File profileImage = new File("src/test/resources/avatar.png");

given().
    baseUri("https://api.example.com").
    multiPart("profile_pic", profileImage).
    multiPart("description", "User avatar upload").
when().
    post("/users/123/upload").
then().
    statusCode(200).
    body("message", equalTo("File uploaded successfully"));
realWorldExample: Testing a KYC (Know Your Customer) process where an API requires uploading an ID document image alongside the user's name and address fields in a single multipart request.
commonMistakes: Using absolute file paths, causing tests to fail on CI/CD servers. Always use relative paths (`src/test/resources`).
Using the wrong form control name. The string passed to `multiPart` must exactly match what the backend expects (e.g., `file`, `avatar`, `document`).
Manually overriding the `Content-Type` with an exact `multipart/form-data` string, which wipes out the boundary parameter automatically generated by REST Assured.
bestPractices: Keep small test files in the repository under `src/test/resources`.
Clean up generated or uploaded data in a `@AfterMethod` if testing a real environment.
Do not manually set the `Content-Type` header when using `multiPart()`; let REST Assured handle the boundary generation.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you test a file upload endpoint using REST Assured?
interviewQuestions.a: You use the `multiPart("fileControlName", new File("path"))` method in the `given()` block, and POST it to the endpoint.

interviewQuestions.q: How do you send a file along with text fields in the same request?
interviewQuestions.a: You can chain multiple `multiPart()` methods. One for the file, and others for the text fields: `multiPart("file", myFile).multiPart("userId", "123")`.

revisionSummary: Use multiPart() to simulate multipart/form-data requests for file uploads.
